text:
HEMATOPATHOLOGY / BONE MARROW BIOPSY REPORT

Patient Name: Margaret Okonkwo
MRN: [REDACTED]
Date of Birth: [REDACTED]
Sex: Female
Ordering Physician: BMT Attending Physician / Hematology-Oncology
Clinical Service: Bone Marrow Transplant Program
Specimen Submitted By: Hematology-Oncology / BMT

------------------------------------------------------------------------
SPECIMEN IDENTIFIER
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen ID: BM-[REDACTED]-22
Accession Number: [REDACTED]
Date of Procedure: [DATE PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
Date Received in Laboratory: [DATE PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
Date of Final Report: [DATE PENDING PROGRAMMATIC INSERTION]
Procedure Location: Outpatient BMT Clinic / Procedure Suite

------------------------------------------------------------------------
CLINICAL INDICATION
------------------------------------------------------------------------
The patient is a 48-year-old female with a history of acute myeloid leukemia with myelodysplasia-related changes (AML-MRC), per 2022 WHO classification, arising from antecedent myelodysplastic syndrome (MDS, RCMD subtype). The patient harbored an adverse-risk mutational profile at diagnosis including FLT3-ITD with high allelic ratio (VAF 0.82), DNMT3A p.R882H, ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12, SRSF2 p.P95H, and RUNX1 p.R204Q, with cytogenetic findings of 46,XX in the dominant clone and a minor del(7q) subclone. She achieved morphologic complete remission with MRD negativity following induction with 7+3 (cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day x7 days plus daunorubicin 60 mg/m2/day x3 days) with midostaurin. She subsequently received one cycle of high-dose cytarabine (HiDAC) consolidation followed by matched unrelated donor allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (10/10 HLA-matched, male unrelated donor, peripheral blood stem cells, CD34+ dose 5.8 x 10^6/kg) with reduced-intensity conditioning (fludarabine/busulfan, pharmacokinetic-targeted IV busulfan) and GVHD prophylaxis consisting of tacrolimus, mini-dose methotrexate, and rabbit anti-thymocyte globulin (ATG, Thymoglobulin).

This bone marrow biopsy is a protocol 6-month post-alloHSCT restaging evaluation (approximately day +180). Clinical status at time of biopsy: patient is well-appearing, ECOG performance status 1, CBC showing WBC 4,800/uL, Hgb 11.9 g/dL, platelets 168,000/uL, with no circulating blasts on differential. She has mild chronic GVHD (NIH Global Severity: Mild) involving skin (nearly resolved on topical clobetasol), oral mucosa (resolving on dexamethasone rinse), and dry eyes (managed with cyclosporine eye drops). Tacrolimus trough: 8.2 ng/mL. CMV PCR: negative.

Clinical questions: (1) Is AML in sustained morphologic complete remission? (2) What is the MRD status by multiparameter flow cytometry? (3) What is the donor chimerism status? (4) Are there any morphologic or cytogenetic features concerning for early relapse, graft failure, or secondary myeloid neoplasm?

------------------------------------------------------------------------
TYPE OF SPECIMEN
------------------------------------------------------------------------
A: Right posterior iliac crest bone marrow core biopsy (trephine)
B: Right posterior iliac crest bone marrow aspirate (bilateral, direct smears and clot section)
C: Peripheral blood (submitted with marrow, for chimerism study)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
GROSS PATHOLOGY DESCRIPTION
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Specimen A (Bone Marrow Core Biopsy): Received in formalin is a cylindrical core of trabecular bone and marrow measuring 1.6 cm in length x 0.2 cm in diameter. The specimen is tan-white to pale pink, firm, and intact. Representative sections are submitted entirely in one cassette (A1) following decalcification with EDTA. Sections stained with H&E, reticulin (Gordon and Sweet silver impregnation), and Prussian blue iron stain.

Specimen B (Bone Marrow Aspirate): Received are six direct-smear slides (Wright-Giemsa stained), a clot section in formalin (submitted in cassette B1), and aspirate in EDTA anticoagulant for flow cytometry immunophenotyping and molecular studies. The direct smears are of adequate cellularity and quality, with particles present. Cell count performed on 500 nucleated cells.

Specimen C (Peripheral Blood): Received is a 4 mL sample of EDTA-anticoagulated peripheral blood submitted for donor chimerism analysis by STR (short tandem repeat) genotyping. Sample is of adequate quality.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
DIAGNOSTIC FINDINGS
------------------------------------------------------------------------

MORPHOLOGY - CORE BIOPSY (H&E):
The core biopsy sections demonstrate normocellular bone marrow with an overall estimated cellularity of 40% (age-adjusted: appropriate for a 48-year-old; slightly lower than prior post-transplant specimens, within expected range for post-chemotherapy/post-transplant marrow). The bony trabeculae are unremarkable without evidence of necrosis, granulomas, or fibrosis. Trilineage hematopoiesis is present and morphologically unremarkable:

- Erythropoiesis: Normoblastic erythropoiesis present in appropriate clusters (erythroid islands). No dyserythropoiesis (no nuclear budding, no megaloblastic change, no ring sideroblasts on iron stain). Erythroid precursors account for approximately 20-25% of marrow nucleated cells.

- Granulopoiesis: Maturing granulopoiesis present throughout the biopsy with normal left shift representation (myeloblasts, promyelocytes, myelocytes, metamyelocytes, bands, and segmented forms). No maturation arrest or architectural distortion. Granulopoiesis accounts for approximately 55-60% of marrow nucleated cells.

- Megakaryopoiesis: Megakaryocytes are present in adequate numbers (approximately 3-5 per high-power field in areas of optimal section). Megakaryocytes are morphologically mature with appropriate nuclear lobation and cytoplasmic granularity. No hypolobation, micromegakaryocytes, or nuclear hyperchromasia to suggest dysplasia. Megakaryocytes are distributed singly and in small loose clusters without abnormal paratrabecular distribution.

- Blasts: No morphologically identifiable blasts in excess of 2% of nucleated marrow cells. No Auer rods identified. CD34 immunostain (see below) shows only rare scattered interstitial CD34+ cells in a non-aggregated, non-clustered distribution consistent with normal marrow CD34+ progenitors.

Reticulin Stain (Gordon and Sweet): MF-0 (no increase in reticulin fibers; resolved from MF-1 at diagnosis). Normal basketweave pattern without linear fibers.

Prussian Blue Iron Stain: Hemosiderin deposits present in macrophages, consistent with adequate iron stores. No ring sideroblasts identified.

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
- CD34: Rare scattered positive cells, estimated <1% of total nucleated cells. No clusters or aggregates of CD34+ blasts. Distribution is consistent with normal hematopoietic progenitors and endothelial cells.
- CD117 (c-KIT): Scattered positivity in mast cells and rare myeloid progenitors. No abnormal aggregates. No increase in CD117+ mast cells.
- MPO (Myeloperoxidase): Highlights maturing granulocytes in expected distribution. No aberrant MPO-negative myeloid population identified.
- CD3: T-lymphocytes present in scattered interstitial and perivascular distribution. No lymphoid aggregates suspicious for post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder (PTLD). No atypical T-cell infiltrates.
- CD20: B-lymphocyte precursors (hematogones) present in small numbers, morphologically appropriate for post-transplant marrow reconstitution. No B-cell lymphoid aggregates or sheets.
- CD138: Rare plasma cells identified, no aggregates.
- TdT: Negative (no increase in immature lymphoid precursors to suggest leukemia recurrence).

MORPHOLOGY - ASPIRATE SMEARS (Wright-Giemsa, 500-cell differential):
The aspirate smears are of adequate quality with visible bone marrow spicules (particles). The 500-cell differential count shows:
  - Myeloblasts: 1.2% (within normal limits; no morphologic features of abnormal blasts)
  - Promyelocytes: 4.6%
  - Myelocytes: 12.8%
  - Metamyelocytes: 11.4%
  - Bands/Segmented neutrophils: 30.2%
  - Eosinophils: 2.4%
  - Basophils: 0.4%
  - Monocytes: 4.2%
  - Erythroid precursors (all stages): 24.8%
  - Lymphocytes: 7.4% (predominantly small mature lymphocytes; no atypical forms)
  - Plasma cells: 0.6%
  - M:E ratio: approximately 2.9:1 (normal)
  - Auer rods: Not identified
  - Dysplasia: None identified in erythroid, myeloid, or megakaryocytic lineages on smear review
  - No abnormal cells, no blasts with atypical cytologic features, no pelgeroid forms

Clot Section: Consistent with aspirate smear morphology. Trilineage hematopoiesis. No increase in blasts.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ANCILLARY STUDIES
------------------------------------------------------------------------

FLOW CYTOMETRY IMMUNOPHENOTYPING (8-color multiparameter, bone marrow aspirate):
Performed by the Clinical Flow Cytometry Laboratory. Instrument: BD FACSCanto II / BD LSR Fortessa. Antibody panels include tube 1 (blast evaluation): CD45, CD34, CD117, CD33, CD13, CD7, HLA-DR, CD123; tube 2 (myeloid/monocyte): CD45, CD64, CD14, CD16, CD11b, CD13, CD33, MPO; tube 3 (erythroid): CD45, CD71, CD235a, CD36, CD105; tube 4 (lymphoid/NK): CD45, CD3, CD4, CD8, CD19, CD56, CD16, CD57; tube 5 (MRD-specific, incorporating patient's diagnostic LAIP): CD45, CD34, CD33, CD7, CD123, CD13, HLA-DR, CD38.

Results:
- Total events acquired: 250,000
- CD34+ blast gate: 0.08% of CD45-positive nucleated events (within normal limits; no immunophenotypic aberrancy)
- Leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) screen: NO LAIP DETECTED. The diagnostic LAIP for this patient (CD34+/CD33+/CD117+/CD7+/CD123+ blast population with aberrant coexpression of CD7) is NOT identified at or above the sensitivity threshold.
- Sensitivity threshold: 0.01% (1 abnormal cell per 10,000 CD45+ nucleated events)
- Aberrant antigen expression: None identified in the myeloid, monocytic, erythroid, or lymphoid populations
- Hematogones (normal B-cell precursors): present, approximately 0.3% of events, consistent with normal post-transplant B-lineage reconstitution
- NK cells: present, 12% of lymphocytes (consistent with early post-transplant immune reconstitution)
- CD4/CD8 ratio: 0.6 (mildly inverted, as expected at 6 months post-alloHSCT; improving)
- Plasma cells: <0.1%, polytypic kappa/lambda ratio

MRD CONCLUSION (Flow Cytometry): NEGATIVE. No evidence of leukemia-associated immunophenotype (LAIP) detected. Sensitivity 0.01%.

DONOR CHIMERISM BY STR (Short Tandem Repeat) GENOTYPING:
Laboratory: Molecular Diagnostics / Transplant Chimerism Laboratory
Method: Capillary electrophoresis-based STR analysis using a validated 15-locus panel (AmpFlSTR Identifiler Plus; internal size standards). Percent donor chimerism calculated as: [donor allele area / (donor allele area + recipient allele area)] x 100. Results reported for informative loci.

Whole Marrow:
  Donor chimerism: 99% donor (range across informative loci: 98.6% - 99.4%)
  Recipient chimerism: 1% (within background/sensitivity range)

T-Cell Fraction (CD3+ sorted cells):
  Donor chimerism: 98% donor
  Recipient T-cell chimerism: 2% (residual recipient T-cells; expected in early immune reconstitution phase)

Myeloid Fraction (CD33+ sorted cells):
  Donor chimerism: 99% donor
  Recipient myeloid chimerism: <1% (below sensitivity threshold)

Interpretation: Full donor engraftment with near-complete donor chimerism in whole marrow, myeloid, and T-cell fractions. No evidence of graft failure or mixed chimerism indicating pending graft rejection. Near-complete donor myeloid chimerism is reassuring against early myeloid relapse from recipient hematopoiesis.

MOLECULAR STUDIES - DIGITAL PCR (ddPCR) MUTATION QUANTIFICATION:
Laboratory: Molecular Hematopathology / Molecular Oncology Laboratory
Indication: Quantitative MRD monitoring for patient-specific mutations identified at diagnosis.

FLT3-ITD (exon 14-15 junction, patient-specific insert length 39 bp) by digital PCR:
  Result: NOT DETECTED
  Assay sensitivity: <0.01% VAF (1 mutant molecule per 10,000 wild-type molecules)
  Copies assessed: [PENDING FINAL QUANTIFICATION; preliminary result negative]
  Interpretation: FLT3-ITD below limit of detection - consistent with MRD-negative status.

DNMT3A p.R882H (c.2645G>A) by allele-specific ddPCR:
  Result: NOT DETECTED
  Assay sensitivity: 0.01% VAF
  Interpretation: DNMT3A R882H mutation not detected. Prior studies at day +30 and day +180 consistently negative. Note: DNMT3A R882H can persist as clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential (CHIP) from donor origin; absence is reassuring.

ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12 (frameshift) by ddPCR:
  Result: NOT DETECTED
  Assay sensitivity: 0.01% VAF
  Interpretation: ASXL1 frameshift mutation below limit of detection.

Note: SRSF2 p.P95H and RUNX1 p.R204Q ddPCR assays are in final validation; results PENDING. Preliminary qualitative PCR screen: both loci appear negative. Final quantitative results will be reported as an addendum upon completion (estimated 5-7 business days).

CYTOGENETICS (Conventional Karyotype):
  Specimen: Bone marrow aspirate in transport medium
  Number of metaphases analyzed: 20
  Result: 46,XY [20/20]
  Interpretation: NORMAL MALE KARYOTYPE. This karyotype reflects 100% donor origin (donor is male; recipient is female). The presence of a 46,XY karyotype in a female recipient confirms complete donor engraftment at the cytogenetic level. No clonal cytogenetic abnormalities identified. Notably, the del(7q) minor clone detected at diagnosis (present in 2/20 metaphases at diagnosis, absent at remission) remains undetectable.

FISH PANEL (Selected Probes, bone marrow interphase cells):
  - CEP7/D7S486 (chromosome 7q31.2 deletion probe): No del(7q) detected (0/200 nuclei; threshold for positivity: >10%)
  - Performed given prior del(7q) subclone at diagnosis. Result: NEGATIVE.
  - FLT3 amplification probe: Not detected.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
IMPRESSION / FINAL DIAGNOSIS
------------------------------------------------------------------------

1. ACUTE MYELOID LEUKEMIA WITH MYELODYSPLASIA-RELATED CHANGES (AML-MRC): SUSTAINED MORPHOLOGIC COMPLETE REMISSION.
   - Bone marrow cellularity 40% with normal trilineage hematopoiesis. Blasts <2% by morphology and flow cytometry.
   - No morphologic evidence of dysplasia in any hematopoietic lineage at this evaluation.
   - No Auer rods. No abnormal blast aggregates.

2. MEASURABLE RESIDUAL DISEASE (MRD) STATUS: NEGATIVE.
   - MRD by 8-color multiparameter flow cytometry (LAIP-based): NEGATIVE (sensitivity 0.01%).
   - FLT3-ITD by digital PCR: NOT DETECTED (sensitivity <0.01% VAF).
   - DNMT3A p.R882H by ddPCR: NOT DETECTED.
   - ASXL1 p.G646Wfs*12 by ddPCR: NOT DETECTED.
   - SRSF2 p.P95H ddPCR: PENDING (addendum to follow).
   - RUNX1 p.R204Q ddPCR: PENDING (addendum to follow).

3. DONOR CHIMERISM: FULL DONOR ENGRAFTMENT CONFIRMED.
   - Whole marrow: 99% donor by STR analysis.
   - T-cell (CD3+): 98% donor.
   - Myeloid (CD33+): 99% donor.
   - Conventional karyotype 46,XY [20/20]: confirms full donor (male) origin. No recipient metaphases detected.

4. NO EVIDENCE OF GRAFT FAILURE, GRAFT REJECTION, OR EVOLVING SECONDARY MYELOID NEOPLASM.
   - Reticulin fibrosis: MF-0 (resolved from MF-1 at original diagnosis).
   - No cytogenetic abnormalities in donor-origin hematopoiesis.
   - del(7q) subclone: not detected by conventional karyotype or FISH.

5. POST-TRANSPLANT IMMUNE RECONSTITUTION: In progress, appropriate for 6-month time point.
   - Hematogones present in small numbers (normal B-lineage reconstitution).
   - NK cells reconstituting (12% of lymphocytes).
   - CD4/CD8 ratio mildly inverted (0.6), expected.

Overall Conclusion: Durable MRD-negative complete remission with full donor chimerism confirmed at 6 months post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant. Excellent molecular clearance of all currently assayed patient-specific driver mutations. No morphologic, immunophenotypic, or molecular evidence of AML relapse. Pending ddPCR results for SRSF2 and RUNX1 will be reported as a separate addendum.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
ATTENDING HEMATOPATHOLOGIST SIGN-OUT
------------------------------------------------------------------------
This report has been reviewed and electronically signed by the attending hematopathologist.
[Electronic Signature Block - REDACTED]
Hematopathology Division
Department of Pathology and Laboratory Medicine

ADDENDUM NOTE: SRSF2 p.P95H and RUNX1 p.R204Q ddPCR results pending. Estimated completion: 5-7 business days from date of specimen receipt. Results will be communicated via addendum report and direct notification to the BMT attending physician. If these assays return as detected above the limit of quantification, clinical correlation and repeat testing will be recommended.

------------------------------------------------------------------------
CORRELATION NOTE
------------------------------------------------------------------------
These findings should be interpreted in the context of the patient's clinical status, peripheral blood counts, and prior marrow assessments. The current biopsy is consistent with the clinical picture of an excellent response to alloHSCT with maintained engraftment. The resolution of reticulin fibrosis (MF-0 vs. MF-1 at diagnosis) further supports durable remission with donor-origin normal hematopoiesis. Correlation with concurrent PET-CT restaging and clinical examination is recommended per institutional BMT protocol.
